title: Helmholtzia novoguineensis
taxonomy: Helmholtzia novoguineensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. It is native to New Guinea and has been classified into the genus Helmholtzia. The plant belongs to the order Commelinales and is further divided into the family Commelinaceae. Within this family, Helmholtzia novoguineensis is placed in the subfamily Commelinoideae. The classification of Helmholtzia novoguineensis is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.

intro: Helmholtzia novoguineensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Hemerocallidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Helmholtzia novoguineensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal effects.
